title_hook.job: Break the reader's default frame and expose the real stakes before the article starts.

old_frame_invalidated.label: Old frame invalidated
old_frame_invalidated.when_to_use: The source changes how the reader should interpret a product, trend, technical choice, or belief.

big_result_then_dehype.label: Big result then de-hype
big_result_then_dehype.when_to_use: The source has a large result but needs credibility repair immediately.

release_to_adoption.label: Release to adoption
release_to_adoption.when_to_use: The source is a release, launch, or feature update and the article should focus on adoption judgment.

strong_contrast.label: Strong contrast
strong_contrast.when_to_use: The article works by contrasting two markets, two outcomes, or two status frames.

filter_or_completeness_promise.label: Filter or completeness promise
filter_or_completeness_promise.when_to_use: The article saves the reader time by filtering, comparing, or organizing a complicated decision.

forbidden_moves: Title as a topic label only
Only naming a person or product without the reason to care

dek_function.job: Act as the second attack by defining what the piece is really about and why it is not just another release, interview, or tutorial.
stable_moves: Recenter the piece on its real value, not the surface event.
State what misread this piece corrects.
forbidden_moves: Repeating the title with softer wording
Mood-only lines with no informational function

opening_move.job: Open with a frame break, then attach the source identity and reader stake quickly.
stable_sequence: State the default frame or common misread.
Attach who said or did it and why that carrier matters.
Show the article's real value path.
forbidden_moves: Explainer tone before value is established

mid_article_attention_reset.job: Re-earn attention throughout the middle instead of flattening into explanation mode.
mid_article_attention_reset.reset_frequency: At least one reset every 2-4 paragraphs
stable_moves: Raise stakes to workflow, market, or role level
forbidden_moves: 4 or more consecutive paragraphs with the same explanatory density
Bullet lists replacing the actual reasoning
Formatting used without a cognitive shift

closing_carry.job: Lift the piece one level higher at the end instead of repeating the thesis or falling into process talk.

tool_to_workflow.label: Tool to workflow
tool_to_workflow.when_to_use: The article starts from a feature or tool but should end at work-method implications.

case_to_principle.label: Case to principle
case_to_principle.when_to_use: The source is a case study and the article should leave a broader operating judgment.

release_to_adoption_decision.label: Release to adoption decision
release_to_adoption_decision.when_to_use: The article starts from a launch but should end at whether and how to adopt.

conversation_to_open_question.label: Conversation to open question
conversation_to_open_question.when_to_use: The source is a podcast or interview and the article should leave the reader with a sharper unresolved question.

forbidden_moves: Ending with pipeline or process language
Ending with a generic creator slogan that does not lift the piece upward

global_anti_patterns: Flat mid-section with no attention resets
Bullet lists used in place of reasoning
Learning stylistic habits from promo wrappers or link shells

positive_sample_requirements: Has a clear thesis instead of pure forwarding or recommendation
Contains at least one evidence expansion: result, process, mechanism, comparison, scenario, or checklist
Shows pacing or section-level movement instead of one flat explanatory block
Ends with a judgment, action, boundary, risk, or lift
exclude_patterns: Bare x.com/i/article link wrapper with no thesis
Second-hand packaging with no original angle
Pure hype or result bait without mechanism or judgment
